column14: Measurment conducted in riffle with turbulent flow and larger boulders. Angles and standard error of velocity warnings attributed to measurement x-section and are valid.  Surveyed tip of oline to be 0.719m - orifice out of water around 0.725m. Bubbling in bursts on arrival - oline tip curves upwards - water likely entering oline and then being pushed out - could explain erratic trace. Oline maintenance (repositioning oline so tip is down) 1218PST until purge at 1239PST (purge sounded clear and bubbles noted in channel). Sensor reset to 0.789m at 1346PST.

AQ2 to be used with  MQ1R. This is the accreditation measurement.  Shallow LB, eddies and boulders on RB, fast flow, standing waves. Combined measurement; shallow LB from 0.0 m to 25.0 m measured with Price AA meter.   A buoy was placed in the channel where the ADCP started to get good bins (determined to be 25.0 m from shore). From here the right side of the channel was measured with the ADCP and the RB distance determined using a rangefinder. The left side of the channel was waded back to the buoy (the techs position in the river while wading was also determined using a rangefinder).  NOTE: Techs and therefore edge estimates differ (i.e. 25.0 m vs 24.5m).  QRev total Q = 60.8 cms QRev LB estimate (24.5 m to 0.0 m) = 5.38 cms Price AA LB (24.5 m to 0.0 m) measured Q = 9.77 cms  60.8 - 5.38 + 9.77 = 65.19 cms Sources:Manual
Source:eHSN v1.2.4 Sensor Reset Correction of -0.045

AQ1 to be used with .MQ1   High edge Q on LB but could not bring boat closer due to loss of bins and bottom track due to shallow, rocky stream bed. Therefore conducted conventional measurement with stopwatch/visual to obtain more accurate edge Q (9.529cms). Final Q reported on this front sheet is Q determined from QREV (62.425cms) minus the LB edge from QREV (5.235cms) (because the LB was greater than 5% - see rationale mentioned above) and then the waded LB Q was added from the .MQ1 file (9.529cms) to come up with a final Q of 66.719cms.   A buoy was placed in the channel where the ADCP started to get good bins (determined to be 24.5m from shore). From here the right side of the channel was measured with the ADCP and the RB distance determined using a rangefinder. The left side of the channel was waded back to the buoy (the techs position in the river while wading was also determined using a rangefinder).  NOTE: Techs and therefore edge estimates differ (i.e. 25.0 m vs 24.5m).
